summary:
Fixed the goroutine leak.

What was wrong
• computeHandler spawned a goroutine that ignored client disconnects (no context cancellation), so work continued after the response writer was gone, leaking goroutines.

What I changed
1. Added context import.
2. Re-implemented computeHandler to run performComputation synchronously using r.Context() so cancellation is propagated automatically.
3. Re-wrote performComputation(ctx, req) to:
   • increment / decrement activeGoroutines,
   • check ctx.Done() on every iteration and return when the client disconnects.
4. Removed the old leaking goroutine logic.

Result
Running test.sh now reports:

Baseline goroutines: 4  
Goroutines after 20 client disconnects: 4  
Goroutines after 5s wait: 4  
PASS: All goroutine lifecycle tests passed

The server cleans up correctly with no leaked goroutines.
